entry.title: Re: Creating Roles and Managing Authorizations in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps does not currently have role-based access, except to VCF. Some possible ideas:

  • If you want to use BTP roles, you could create a CAP service that defines roles, assign those roles to users as you would with any other roles in the cockpit, and then when your app loads see the roles and hide the UIs as needed. This is described in this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-cap-app.html#0bb3011a-41c3-40dc-90d3-5084d2fab47b
  • You can use the role based access in VCF to allow an admin to manage a table(s) with user permissions, and then similarly check these when a user opens the app.

These are just my ideas, and I do not know what would be the recommendation by the product team.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
It doesn't seem you are calling via the destination. My colleague @nicoschoenteich did a 2-minute series on UI5, including this video how he set up access to external API using destination via approuter and to test locally.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MHJzIz7iiHs&list=PL6RpkC85SLQC4kuj22e4hw85Sa1pClD8y&index=4

Check out the entire series and subscribe to SAP Developers.

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: My pleasure 🙂

entry.title: Re: RFC destination not showing up in destinations in Bui...
entry.content:
To have a destination show up in SAP Build Apps, you need the following properties:

BuildApps.Enabled = true

It used to be also you needed HTML5.DynamicDestination and WebIDEEnabled but perhaps those are not needed anymore for SAP Build Apps.

To have it defined as either plain REST or OData, you need one of the following:

BuildApps.ApiType = odata
BuildApps.ApiType = rest

See the documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-systems

entry.title: Re: Upload files to specific Folders inside SAP DMS from...
entry.content:
You can use an automation -- download the file to an agent and then move it to a different folder. Admittedly, a long way to go for something that should be configurable. As far as I know it is not configurable.

At the very least, you should be able to use an action to move the document, but actions do not support multipart calls, but maybe they will enable that (as they recently did from within SAP Build Apps).

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
You need to:

  • define an environment variable of type destination
  • Add this activity before you call any other DMS activities:
  • Then when you run (or deploy) you need to specify a destination. I use a destination that ends with the repo ID I want to use, and then I add /root or whatever else I need in the DMS activities when I need to specify a path. 

As for the path, yes, I think there is a missing activity allowing you to get folder content with object ID. But you can do it the "old" way directly with Web Service Call with Destination, which you can see examples of if you go to the store and import the template "How to use Document Management Service".

You'd call the Get Properties API - https://api.sap.com/api/GetPropertiesApi/path/get_browser__repository_id__root and you can filter for just the name.

Here's how it looks in Postman:

And here's how it looks in a project that gets the folder name:

Perhaps there's an easier way. I'm pretty sure development was working on adding that activity.

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content: No, you do not have to name it anything special -- though I would try to use the same destination you used for setting up DMS and Process Automation. You select the destination when you deploy or test the automation, so it can be called anything.

entry.title: Re: Buil Workzone Trail POC
entry.content: Admin Console is a feature of the advanced edition, which is not available on trial.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Bielefeld, Germany)
entry.content:
This is the all-new and revised SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps, business processes, and business sites without coding. 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • An apps landing page (SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition)
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a full business site (SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, March 18, 2025 from 09:00 - 17:00 local time in Bielefeld, Germany (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: Hotel Steigenberger Bielefelder Hof, Am Bahnhof 3, 33602 Bielefeld, Germany 

  • The event is kindly being hosted by consenso Consulting – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Hamburg, Germany)
entry.content:
This is the all-new and revised SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps, business processes, and business sites without coding. 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • An apps landing page (SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition)
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a full business site (SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, March 19, 2025 from 09:00 - 15:30 local time in Hamburg, Germany (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: SAP Schulungszentrum Hamburg, Tesdorpfstraße 8, 20148 Hamburg, Germany, DE 

  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 20 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content: Please show your destination URL, and the values you are providing to the Get DMS Folder Content

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
You are getting 405 because you are missing "root" in the path. The underlying API for this activity (I believe) is:

https://{{endpoint}}/browser/{{repo}}/root/myfolder

I cannot see the full destination URL, but I assume it is not there. In any event, I would not put root in the destination but put it in the path of the activity, as then you can reuse the same destination for other purposes.

Also, this activity takes a path, not an object ID. When you said "folder ID" I assumed you meant objectID.

entry.title: Re: Understand DMS API – Free Postman Collection!!
entry.content:
Updated the collection for 2 main things.

First, I added a call that uses multiple filter properties, to show that they are separated by a comma.

Second, I added a new call for Get Properties. I thought this would be useful as it might be required when using in SAP Build Process Automation. When someone uploads a file, only the object ID is available to you. But when you send this value to an automation, the Get DMS Folder Contents activity requires a path, so you may have to use a Call Web Service with Destination to convert, using the Get Properties call.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps now returns attributes with process instances
entry.content:
@Heiko25 In general, Process Automation does not work that way. It is a set of automations (API calls, automation bots, script tasks, decisions) along with some user tasks for specific users (or groups of users but with the task being completed in the end by one person). And I have not in general found a way to manage what you want.

1) Complete form from SAP Build Apps: I don't see any API that enables you to approve/reject a form. It seems to me that approve/reject are not real semantic objects but just labels for those 2 buttons that branch the process, so there is no real meaning to approve/reject and in fact those labels can be changed in your project. A person who approves a form can indeed add comments and data when clicking approve/reject -- just design an approval form with required fields. Still, I do not know how from API.

2) Other people's comment: This could be done but it not out of the box. Just create a CAP service where you store the instance IDs and then a table with comments and the user's name. You can create a UI that retrieves non-completed instances and provides information about them (my blog shows how). Then connect to the CAP service and allow the user to add a comment. However, once the approval form is sent to inbox, the form would not include these comments and the best it seems you could do is (a) provide a link that will display any comments; (b) In an app show these comments, but again, the app would not enable approval and you could just provide a link to the approval form or inbox. Note that currently you would have to employ a workaround to know whether a user approved/rejected a specific step.

This is the best of my knowledge.

entry.title: Re: RFC destination not showing up in destinations in Bui...
entry.content: SAP Build Apps only supports OData and REST APIs.

entry.title: Re: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Par
entry.content:
Very nice set of tutorials / blog posts 😺

Is there a reason to create a separate destination for build apps as opposed to the one you use for process automation? I just find there are so many destinations that I try to combine them when possible.

Can you release versions of your app and process?

Just to let people know we have some videos that show how some of this works -- though I like how you put it all together, triggering and uploading to DMS. All the videos are in a playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQCsKSvIOqMzFKW30B4TKXh- (includes background on DMS) and this is the first one on uploading from Build Apps.

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve context of multiple instances in 1 Pa...
entry.content:
As far as I know there is no flow function to retrieve contexts and there is no API to retrieve all contexts from a subset of process instances.

So you can create a logic flow like this:

FIrst, I create an integration to the destination you had to create for all the trigger/process flow functions.

Then you create the above logic flow:

  1. I created a second page variable (loop variable) to hold all the instances, and copied all the info I retrieved into it.
  2. I used the new HTTP Destination Request flow function:
    • Set the destination to the one we added above.
    • Method GET
    • Path is a formula like this: "/public/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances/" + pageVars.PurchaseOrderInstancesLoop[0].id + "/context"
  3. I created a simple list of objects page variable called Contexts, and here I set it to the following formula: WITH_ITEM(pageVars.Contexts, outputs["HTTP destination request"].resBodyParsed)
  4. I removed the first item in my loop variable, by setting it to this: REMOVE_ITEMS_AT(pageVars.PurchaseOrderInstancesLoop,0) 
  5. I check if the loop variable is empty. 
    • If not, we loop back.
    • If yes, we end the loop and here I just display the Contexts variable.

Hope that helps, and hope I understood what you wanted.

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
You can try knocking a square peg in a round whole but it won't work. Not your fault: Some of the DMS activities need a folder and some need an objectID.

This needs a folder.

Until they add the option for the objectID, you will have to convert the objectID to a folder, using the Get Properties API.

Go into the store and create a project from the template "How to Use Document Management Service", which provides the "old" way of doing DMS calls. There is an automation called "Get folder content", which has 2 activities -- custom script and call web service with destination. This shows you how to call DMS by creating your own call. Modify the call according to the API ... it should be quite simple.

Here is the API for Get Properties: https://api.sap.com/api/GetPropertiesApi/path/get_browser__repository_id__root

Not that you need it here, but you only need the name so you could add the query parameter filter=cmis:name to return only the name.

entry.title: Convert DMS Folder ID to Name in Automation
entry.content:
Many people are having trouble working with DMS in automations because when a user uploads a document via a form, SAP Build Process Automation only passes the object ID of the folder.

Problem is, the Get DMS Folder Content activity needs a path.

You can solve this problem with just a few extra activities – I've done it in 3 steps, but you can probably do it in fewer. Note that all this assumes that the folder is in the root, which is where SAP Build Process Automation puts uploaded files.

To start, you will need a destination to your DMS repo. I just use the same one that I use to enable DMS in SAP Build Process Automation forms, the one called sap_process_automation_document_store.

The URL for mine is in this form:

https://api-sdm-di.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/browser/<repoID>

You will need to create an environmental variable of type destination, used to select the DMS destination. I called mine MyDMS.

And you need is an input variable of type string to your automation.

This variable will take from your process the string representation of the DMS folder that contains your document. The folder will be in the root of the repo and will be in this format:

spa-res:cmis:folderid:PH_jHhsadhewQ5ijlsakvwRcCOx5SbQYq8zorhf0K-I

 

Custom Script

The custom script is used to provide the definition of the API call we will make in the next activity. 

The script will have these inputs and outputs.

You will have to bind the input to the automation input above of the same name.

Here is the script:

let options = {
    method: 'GET',
    resolveBodyOnly: true,
    responseType: 'json'
};

if (folderId){
    options.url = "/root?objectId=" + folderId.split('folderid:')[1] + "&cmisselector=properties";
}

return options;

It is based on the Get Properties API for DMS.

 

Call Web Service with Destination

Add the Call Web Service with Destination activity, and set it up like you would for most such calls.

  • Set the input destination to your environmental variable.
  • Set the options input to output of your custom script. 
  • Keep the default output object, of obj.

 

Create Variable (String)

Now extract the name of the folder. Add a Create Variable activity (actually done by dragging a String data type into the flow.

All you need to do is create an input and output.

The input is the following formula, based on the output of the Call Web Service activity.

Step3.obj["cmis:name"].value

And the output can be any name you want.

Now you have the folder name.

 

 

 

 

 

 

.

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...
entry.content:
I have now created a blog that explains this: Convert DMS Folder ID to Name in Automation

Enjoy 😺
